CandidateBinding.description:
How to call a candidate.

MLI-166 confirmed all 10 dev-account deployments — OpenAI-family AND
serverless — serve via a single Azure OpenAI route shape, so a single
binding shape suffices for v1. The binding plugin reads these fields; the
actual API key is resolved from Key Vault by `key_vault_secret_name`, never
embedded here.

api_version.description: Azure OpenAI / Foundry API version pin
api_version.title: Api Version

auth_method.description: How the binding plugin authenticates; 'api_key_header' is the v1 default
auth_method.title: Auth Method

key_vault_secret_name.description: Key Vault secret name holding the API key — never the key itself
key_vault_secret_name.title: Key Vault Secret Name

CandidateBinding.title: CandidateBinding

CandidateFamily.description:
Distinguishes reasoning models (whose output carries `reasoning_content`
alongside `content`) from chat models. Evaluators consult this to decide
whether a response's reasoning trace is in scope. See MLI-165 §2.
CandidateFamily.title: CandidateFamily

CandidateStatus.description:
Portfolio state. Promotion to a primary/fallback requires a written
rationale (recorded out-of-band on the matrix run audit log).
CandidateStatus.title: CandidateStatus

description: A model candidate in the slate.

max_tokens.description: Required, no default. Reasoning-class candidates need budget headroom for the reasoning trace before visible content emits — see MLI-165 §1.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

tiers.description: Tiers this candidate is being evaluated against
tiers.title: Tiers

title: Candidate
